codeReview.summary: Reviewed the alias-aware analysis entity resolution changes in scripts and matrix surfaces against the feature contract and added regression coverage. I found no blocking or non-blocking correctness issues in the committed implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Alias-aware entity matching is now implemented via duplicated helper logic in both `src/ui/pages/scripts.py` and `src/ui/pages/matrix.py`, but the shared library/guidance does not document a reusable pattern or common helper location for canonical-name-or-alias resolution even though this feature's preconditions called for centralized logic.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/ui/pages/scripts.py` adds `_resolve_character_name` / `_resolve_location_name` plus handled helpers, while `src/ui/pages/matrix.py` adds separate `_is_character_handled` / `_is_location_handled` implementations with the same canonical-or-alias matching behavior. `.factory/library/patterns.md` and mission `AGENTS.md` document tested pure helpers but do not mention a shared alias-resolution pattern.

summary: Pass. The feature satisfies the requested behavior: reopened analysis/pending-entity checks now treat merged aliases as handled, and direct-fact deduction creation resolves aliases to existing canonical entities. Regression tests directly cover both claimed behaviors, and I did not identify correctness or regression defects in the reviewed diff.
